problems.problem_statement: What is artificial intelligence? Give a one-sentence definition.
problems.rubric: Must mention machines performing tasks that typically require human intelligence.

problems.problem_statement: How does machine learning differ from a rule-based system?
problems.rubric: ML learns from data; rule-based uses hand-coded rules.

problems.problem_statement: What is supervised learning? Give an example.
problems.rubric: Learning from labeled examples; e.g. spam classification.

problems.problem_statement: What is unsupervised learning? Give an example.
problems.rubric: Finding patterns without labels; e.g. clustering.

problems.problem_statement: What role does training data play in ML?
problems.rubric: Model learns patterns from this data.

problems.problem_statement: What is overfitting? Why is it a problem?
problems.rubric: Model fits training data too closely and fails to generalize.

problems.problem_statement: What is underfitting?
problems.rubric: Model too simple; high bias, poor performance.

problems.problem_statement: How can bias in data affect an AI system?
problems.rubric: Model can perpetuate or amplify bias in data.

problems.problem_statement: What is accuracy as a metric? When might it be misleading?
problems.rubric: Correct/total; misleading with imbalanced classes.

problems.problem_statement: When would you care more about precision than recall?
problems.rubric: When false positives are costly (e.g. spam).

problems.problem_statement: What is fairness in AI? Give one example of unfairness.
problems.rubric: Treating groups equitably; e.g. biased hiring tool.

problems.problem_statement: Why is transparency important in AI?
problems.rubric: Accountability, trust, debugging, regulation.

problems.problem_statement: How can AI systems affect privacy?
problems.rubric: Data collection, inference, re-identification.

problems.problem_statement: What is reinforcement learning in one sentence?
problems.rubric: Learning via rewards/penalties from environment.

problems.problem_statement: What are neural networks (high level)?
problems.rubric: Layers of units with weights; learn from data.

problems.problem_statement: What is NLP? Give one application.
problems.rubric: Processing human language; e.g. translation, chatbots.

problems.problem_statement: What is computer vision? Give one application.
problems.rubric: Understanding images/video; e.g. face recognition.

problems.problem_statement: How do recommendation systems work (in brief)?
problems.rubric: Use past behavior or similarity to suggest items.

problems.problem_statement: What are limits of current AI?
problems.rubric: Narrow tasks, no common sense, no consciousness.

problems.problem_statement: Give an example of human-AI collaboration.
problems.rubric: e.g. doctor + diagnostic AI, driver + assist.

problems.problem_statement: Explain: why is 'AI has feelings' a misconception?
problems.rubric: Current AI has no subjective experience.

problems.problem_statement: When is more data not better?
problems.rubric: Biased data, noisy data, wrong distribution.

problems.problem_statement: Name one way to improve fairness in an AI system.
problems.rubric: Diverse data, fairness metrics, auditing.

problems.problem_statement: What is explainable AI and why might we need it?
problems.rubric: Understanding why model made a decision; trust, law.

problems.problem_statement: Distinguish narrow AI from general AI.
problems.rubric: Narrow: specific tasks. General: human-like breadth (not yet).

problems.problem_statement: What is prompt engineering? Why does it matter?
problems.rubric: Designing inputs to get desired outputs from LLMs.

problems.problem_statement: In one sentence: what is a large language model?
problems.rubric: Neural model trained on text to predict/generate text.

problems.problem_statement: What is generative AI? Give two examples.
problems.rubric: AI that creates new content; e.g. text, images, code.

problems.problem_statement: What is hallucination in the context of LLMs?
problems.rubric: Model producing confident but false or nonsensical output.

problems.problem_statement: Name two limitations of current LLMs.
problems.rubric: e.g. hallucination, no grounding, no guarantee of correctness.

problems.problem_statement: What does responsible AI mean?
problems.rubric: Fair, transparent, accountable, safe design and deployment.

problems.problem_statement: Give one best practice when writing prompts.
problems.rubric: e.g. be specific, provide context, give examples.

problems.problem_statement: Why is AI safety a concern?
problems.rubric: Misuse, unintended behavior, alignment, security.

problems.problem_statement: Describe one real-world AI ethics case (e.g. bias in hiring).
problems.rubric: Identify issue, stakeholders, and possible mitigation.
